title: Nocturnals — Yen Yu-Ting Solo Exhibition (Bio, Press Release, Artist Statement)
source: YIYUN ART press release and artist's own bio/statement for the 2026 solo exhibition Nocturnals

cards.definition: a starting point for a discussion, exploration, or piece of work
cards.zh: 出發點；（討論或創作的）起點
cards.sourceSentence: Often taking images and texts from news events as her point of departure, Yen brings together personal perception with a transformed vocabulary of traditional ink painting.
examples: The professor used a single photograph as a point of departure for the whole lecture.
Their shared childhood in the countryside became a point of departure for the novel.

cards.definition: a state of anxiety, nervous excitement, or turmoil
cards.sourceSentence: Through this process, she recomposes information and narratives from different times and places, using painting to reflect a collective state of agitation and disorientation in contemporary society, as well as the absurdity and subtle humor that emerge from it.
examples: He paced the room in visible agitation before the interview.
The news caused a wave of agitation among investors.

cards.definition: a state of confusion about one's surroundings, direction, or sense of identity
cards.sourceSentence: Through this process, she recomposes information and narratives from different times and places, using painting to reflect a collective state of agitation and disorientation in contemporary society, as well as the absurdity and subtle humor that emerge from it.
examples: Jet lag left her with a lingering sense of disorientation.
The sudden move to a new country caused a period of disorientation for the whole family.

cards.definition: the quality of being wildly unreasonable, illogical, or inappropriate
cards.sourceSentence: Through this process, she recomposes information and narratives from different times and places, using painting to reflect a collective state of agitation and disorientation in contemporary society, as well as the absurdity and subtle humor that emerge from it.
examples: The comedy highlights the absurdity of modern bureaucracy.
She laughed at the sheer absurdity of the situation.

cards.definition: strange or mysterious in an unsettling way
cards.zh: 詭異的；不可思議地令人不安的
cards.sourceSentence: Through her distinctive approach to ink, colored pencil, and other media, Yen renders a series of uncanny scenes encountered while wandering through a forest at night.
examples: There was an uncanny silence in the abandoned house.
He bears an uncanny resemblance to his grandfather.

cards.definition: to completely cover, surround, or enclose something
cards.sourceSentence: Everything here is enveloped in darkness; the field of vision narrows sharply.
examples: Fog enveloped the harbor by early morning.
A sense of calm enveloped the room after the announcement.

cards.definition: the entire area that can be seen without moving one's eyes or turning one's head
cards.sourceSentence: Everything here is enveloped in darkness; the field of vision narrows sharply.
examples: A cyclist suddenly entered my field of vision.
Wide-angle lenses expand the camera's field of vision.

cards.definition: to understand something fully, or to seize and hold something firmly
cards.sourceSentence: No matter how one looks around, the forest answers only with depth and silence, remaining almost impossible to grasp.
examples: It took years for her to fully grasp the theory.
He grasped the rope just before he slipped.

cards.definition: to stop moving or happening
cards.sourceSentence: Then, all at once, the traveler comes to a halt: something seems to be blocking the path ahead.
examples: Traffic came to a halt after the accident.
Production came to a halt when the machine broke down.

cards.definition: an upright stone slab or pillar, typically inscribed, used as a monument or marker
cards.sourceSentence: Unknown ruined steles and statues stand on their own in a wild clearing, surrounded by withered trees.
examples: Archaeologists uncovered a stele carved with an ancient king's decree.
The cemetery's oldest stele has been worn smooth by centuries of rain.

cards.definition: an open area within a forest or wooded region, free of trees
cards.sourceSentence: Unknown ruined steles and statues stand on their own in a wild clearing, surrounded by withered trees.
examples: They pitched their tent in a small clearing by the river.
Deer often gather in the clearing at dusk.

cards.definition: dried up, shriveled, or having lost vitality, especially from age or lack of water
cards.sourceSentence: Unknown ruined steles and statues stand on their own in a wild clearing, surrounded by withered trees.
examples: A withered bouquet still sat on the windowsill.
The withered old man walked slowly with a cane.

cards.definition: to cut off or separate something completely
cards.sourceSentence: These relics are objects severed from their original context and meaning.
examples: The company severed all ties with its former supplier.
A single blow severed the rope.

cards.definition: something that has been discarded or rejected as no longer useful or wanted
cards.sourceSentence: As the artist writes, "They resemble monuments left behind by history, yet also look like defective cast-offs casually discarded by a molding factory."
examples: The thrift store was full of other people's cast-offs.
He wore his older brother's cast-off jacket.

cards.definition: to grow or develop vigorously; to thrive
cards.sourceSentence: Around them, trees grow freely, moving through cycles of flourishing and decay.
examples: The business flourished after expanding online.
Wildflowers flourish in the untouched meadow every spring.

cards.definition: to take great pleasure or delight in something
cards.sourceSentence: A new composite form is born, quietly reveling in itself
examples: She revels in the freedom of working from home.
The children reveled in the first snow of winter.

cards.definition: a disruption or displacement from a normal or expected position or order
cards.zh: 錯位；脫節（比喻秩序被打亂）
cards.sourceSentence: The uncanniness produced by these dislocations interrupts the habitual assumptions through which contemporary viewers tend to recognize and categorize images.
examples: The film explores the dislocation felt by immigrants in a new country.
Economic dislocation followed the sudden factory closures.

cards.definition: done constantly or repeatedly as a habit; usual
cards.sourceSentence: The uncanniness produced by these dislocations interrupts the habitual assumptions through which contemporary viewers tend to recognize and categorize images.
examples: He arrived at his habitual seat by the window.
Her habitual optimism helped the team through hard times.

cards.definition: a representative sample or slice that reveals the internal structure or variety of something
cards.zh: 橫切面；具代表性的樣本
cards.sourceSentence: Like a cross-section, it reveals the uneven and entangled details of contemporary perception beneath the appearance of a unified whole.
examples: The survey included a broad cross-section of the population.
A cross-section of the tree trunk showed its growth rings.

cards.definition: twisted together in a confused or complicated way
cards.sourceSentence: Like a cross-section, it reveals the uneven and entangled details of contemporary perception beneath the appearance of a unified whole.
examples: The fishing lines became hopelessly entangled.
Their finances were deeply entangled with the failing company's.

cards.definition: strange or unusual; or distinctively characteristic of a particular thing
cards.sourceSentence: In response to the disorder and constant change of the present, as well as the peculiar psychological conditions of contemporary life, Yen Yu-Ting has developed a highly personal form of painterly cunfa.
examples: There was something peculiar about the way he avoided eye contact.
Each region has its own peculiar customs.

cards.definition: to plan or map out a course of action or development
cards.sourceSentence: At the level of meaning, her practice continues the traditional idea of the unity of calligraphy and painting, while charting a distinctly different path forward.
examples: The memoir charts her journey from poverty to success.
The company is charting a new direction after the merger.

cards.definition: a process of investigation or systematic exploration into a subject
cards.sourceSentence: Looking back across Yen's artistic development, one can trace the beginning of this painterly inquiry to her 2018 residency at the Gwangju Museum of Art in Korea.
examples: Her scientific inquiry led to a surprising discovery.
The book is a philosophical inquiry into the nature of time.

cards.definition: a period during which an artist works at a particular institution, often with support and space provided
cards.zh: 駐村（藝術家駐地創作計畫）
cards.sourceSentence: Looking back across Yen's artistic development, one can trace the beginning of this painterly inquiry to her 2018 residency at the Gwangju Museum of Art in Korea.
examples: She spent six months on an artist residency in Berlin.
The museum offers a summer residency for emerging painters.

cards.definition: smoothly changing or flowing; not fixed or rigid
cards.sourceSentence: In this latest presentation, the language has reached a greater maturity, becoming more fluid, flexible, and richly layered in its capacity for close reading.
examples: Her dance moves were fluid and effortless.
The company's plans remain fluid as the market shifts.

cards.definition: a careful, detailed analysis of a text or image, attentive to its language, structure, and nuance
cards.zh: 細讀；深入細緻的文本（或圖像）分析
cards.sourceSentence: In this latest presentation, the language has reached a greater maturity, becoming more fluid, flexible, and richly layered in its capacity for close reading.
examples: The seminar was devoted to a close reading of the poem's final stanza.
A close reading of the painting reveals hidden symbols in the background.

cards.definition: to begin a journey, task, or undertaking
cards.sourceSentence: For those who dare to set out into the deep of night, the clues leading toward a new world may be found everywhere.
examples: They set out at dawn to reach the summit by noon.
She set out to write a novel that would change people's minds.

cards.definition: a recurring image, theme, or idea in a work of art or literature
cards.zh: （藝術、文學中反覆出現的）主題、意象
cards.sourceSentence: Taking the traveler, ruins, and withered trees as recurring motifs, I construct a series of encounters that feel strangely familiar, yet remain difficult to name.
examples: Water is a recurring motif throughout the poet's work.
The film repeats the motif of a closing door at key turning points.

cards.definition: an excessive amount of something, more than can be easily processed or handled
cards.sourceSentence: Continuing my recent reflections on news, memory, and painting, I borrow from and translate the language of traditional ink painting in order to capture the observations and sensations I experience in the present: an age of information overload, and of fragmented states of mind and memory.
examples: Constant notifications can cause serious information overload.
The server crashed from an overload of requests.

cards.definition: broken into disconnected or incomplete pieces
cards.sourceSentence: Continuing my recent reflections on news, memory, and painting, I borrow from and translate the language of traditional ink painting in order to capture the observations and sensations I experience in the present: an age of information overload, and of fragmented states of mind and memory.
examples: Her fragmented memories of the accident made it hard to testify.
The report paints a fragmented picture of the region's history.

cards.definition: a single, all-encompassing story or theory that claims to explain a broad range of events or experiences
cards.zh: 宏大敘事（涵蓋一切的整體性論述）
cards.sourceSentence: For me, Nocturnals is not meant to build a grand or complete narrative.
examples: The exhibition deliberately avoids any grand narrative about the war.
Postmodern thinkers are often skeptical of grand narratives.

cards.definition: to put the parts of something back together again
cards.sourceSentence: Rather, it is a series of active attempts—made along the paths of painting and everyday life—to observe, gather, take apart, transform, and reassemble.
examples: It took an hour to reassemble the bookshelf after moving.
The historian reassembled the events from scattered letters and diaries.

cards.definition: to separate something into its individual pieces; to analyze something critically
cards.zh: 拆解；（比喻）仔細拆解分析
cards.sourceSentence: Rather, it is a series of active attempts—made along the paths of painting and everyday life—to observe, gather, take apart, transform, and reassemble.
examples: He took apart the old radio to see how it worked.
The critic took the film apart scene by scene.

cards.definition: to twist together, or to become closely connected
cards.sourceSentence: Instead, through layer upon layer of transcription, vast amounts of contemporary information scatter, intertwine, and accumulate over time.
examples: Their fates became intertwined after the accident.
Vines intertwined around the old fence.

cards.definition: an image or impression made by placing paper over a textured surface and rubbing it with pencil, wax, or chalk
cards.sourceSentence: In the end, the specific content of the written words is transformed into a texture resembling a rubbing of bronze and stone inscriptions, becoming a kind of sediment of contemporary memory.
examples: Students made a rubbing of the old gravestone to study its carving.
The museum displayed a rubbing taken from an ancient bronze vessel.

cards.definition: words or symbols carved, engraved, or written onto a hard surface
cards.sourceSentence: In the end, the specific content of the written words is transformed into a texture resembling a rubbing of bronze and stone inscriptions, becoming a kind of sediment of contemporary memory.
examples: The ring bore a tiny inscription with her initials.
Archaeologists deciphered the inscription on the ancient tomb.

cards.definition: matter that settles at the bottom of a liquid; (figurative) a layer or residue left behind over time
cards.zh: 沉澱物；（比喻）積澱下來的痕跡
cards.sourceSentence: In the end, the specific content of the written words is transformed into a texture resembling a rubbing of bronze and stone inscriptions, becoming a kind of sediment of contemporary memory.
examples: Fine sediment collected at the bottom of the wine bottle.
Old letters felt like a sediment of forgotten emotions.

cards.definition: the fact of placing two things side by side, especially to compare or contrast them
cards.sourceSentence: Taking as a point of departure the sense of nostalgia and the dialogue between past and present created by the juxtaposition of the traveler, the stele, and the withered tree in ink painting, I construct a site of ruins that crosses time, space, and culture.
examples: The exhibit's power comes from the juxtaposition of old photographs and new digital art.
The juxtaposition of comedy and tragedy is a hallmark of his films.

cards.definition: a sentimental longing or affection for the past
cards.sourceSentence: Taking as a point of departure the sense of nostalgia and the dialogue between past and present created by the juxtaposition of the traveler, the stele, and the withered tree in ink painting, I construct a site of ruins that crosses time, space, and culture.
examples: The old song filled her with nostalgia for her college years.
The film relies heavily on nostalgia for 1980s pop culture.

cards.definition: the process of moving through or elapsing over time
cards.sourceSentence: Within this imagined site of ruins, the stele points to the formation of order, consensus, and history; the withered tree suggests the ways memory changes, declines, and returns over time; and the traveler—that is, the viewer in the exhibition space—becomes a passing witness, sensing the passage of time and the rise and fall of memory and history.
examples: The passage of time slowly wore down the old stone steps.
Their friendship survived the passage of many difficult years.

cards.definition: a small remaining part or quantity of something
cards.sourceSentence: At the same time, I try to construct nameless remnants of many different forms through a playful and paradoxical method of assemblage.
examples: Only a few remnants of the ancient wall still stand.
She sewed a quilt from remnants of old fabric.

cards.definition: seemingly contradictory or absurd, yet possibly true
cards.sourceSentence: At the same time, I try to construct nameless remnants of many different forms through a playful and paradoxical method of assemblage.
examples: It's paradoxical that the safest cars often feel the most dangerous to drive.
There is something paradoxical about seeking solitude in a crowded city.

cards.definition: a work of art made by grouping together found or unrelated objects; a collection or gathering
cards.sourceSentence: At the same time, I try to construct nameless remnants of many different forms through a playful and paradoxical method of assemblage.
examples: The sculpture is an assemblage of scrap metal and broken glass.
The exhibit featured an assemblage of everyday objects turned into art.

cards.definition: the act or result of joining separate things together, especially in a way that seems artificial or forced
cards.zh: 嫁接（比喻：拼接不同事物）
cards.sourceSentence: What interests me more is the process of testing out various ambiguous graftings, and of placing within them clues for looking and gaps for imagination.
examples: The novel is a strange graft of memoir and science fiction.
Critics called the remake an awkward graft of two unrelated genres.

cards.definition: a place or means by which someone begins to engage with or understand something
cards.sourceSentence: The purpose of painting is not to offer answers or explanations, but to provide, from my point of view, an entry point into the shifting relationship between time, space, and history.
examples: The introduction offers a gentle entry point for readers new to the subject.
Her personal story serves as an entry point into the larger historical events.
